note: Trigger-accuracy evaluation suite. Run via skill-creator:skill-creator to measure trigger/non-trigger correctness and iterate on description. Target: >=80% true-positive rate, <=5% false-positive rate.

cases.prompt: ship a keyboard shortcut help modal end-to-end — run the whole CE pipeline
cases.rationale: Explicit request for end-to-end CE pipeline with unspecified stage; classic bare-idea entry point.

cases.prompt: take docs/plans/2026-04-15-002-feat-search-plan.md and run it through to PR
cases.rationale: Plan path + 'run it through to PR' signals downstream orchestration; plan-approval gate still applies.

cases.prompt: streak counter resets to 0 after midnight UTC — diagnose, fix, review, open PR
cases.rationale: Bug description + multi-stage workflow (diagnose → fix → review → PR) matches the debug-input path with systematic-debugging preroll.
BMAD story path + 'compound engineering' → v2 story-to-brief bridge, then CE pipeline.

cases.prompt: run every.to's compound engineering workflow on this idea: dark-mode toggle
cases.rationale: 'every.to compound engineering workflow' is a direct reference to the pipeline this skill chains.

cases.prompt: run the CE pipeline headless from a shell script and give me the PR URL
cases.rationale: Headless mode is a v2 feature of this skill specifically — JSON return contract for programmatic use.

cases.prompt: ship 'add library continue-listening' in autopilot — only stop me for plan approval
cases.rationale: v3 --autopilot flag explicitly preserves the plan gate. Request matches the semantics this skill owns.

cases.prompt: take docs/brainstorms/2026-04-10-search-refactor-requirements.md through plan + work + review + PR
cases.rationale: Requirements doc + multi-stage chain = entry at /ce:plan. v2 classifier handles this shape.

cases.prompt: run this plan end-to-end with cross-model review from openai and glm
cases.rationale: Cross-model review flag is specific to ce-orchestrator; no other skill chains plan→work→review and supports it.

cases.prompt: brainstorm ideas for improving the course-import flow
cases.rationale: Single-stage request — user wants /ce-brainstorm directly, not the full pipeline.

cases.prompt: write a plan for adding dark mode
cases.rationale: Explicit single-stage: planning only. No pipeline signaled.
Review-only request. ce-orchestrator would overreach — it would re-plan, re-work, and re-review.
Unrelated utility request — must not over-trigger.
User explicitly named /lfg. Should not hijack — ce-orchestrator only triggers when user asks for the full pipeline.

cases.prompt: execute epic E92 from start to finish across all stories
cases.rationale: Epic-level multi-story work is epic-orchestrator's domain. ce-orchestrator is single-story per invocation.
User explicitly invoked BMAD /start-story. ce-orchestrator should not preempt.

cases.prompt: document the lessons from shipping the search refactor
cases.rationale: Post-merge lessons documentation is /ce-compound's job. ce-orchestrator defers to it via reminder, doesn't replace it.

assertions.description: Run via `python -m scripts.run_loop` (skill-creator). Targets below are v3 floor; description tuning should push higher.
knownFailureModes: Confusion with /lfg — both chain plan→work→review. Disambiguation depends on upstream-stage signals (brainstorm/ideate) or explicit 'every.to' phrasing.

Confusion with epic-orchestrator — user requests covering 'ship this story' may match both. Epic-orchestrator wins when input is an epic ID; ce-orchestrator wins when input is a single idea/plan/story.
Over-triggering on 'compound engineering' phrase alone — the phrase is semantic, not contractual. Description must emphasize end-to-end pipeline, not just the words.
